title: City of Reno Boards and Commissions 2025 Q3 Vacancy Report
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: January 11, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Tyler Shaw, Agenda Manager
  Cali Shy, Agenda Coordinator
DEPT: City Manager's Office
SUBJECT: City of Reno Boards and Commissions 2025 Q3 Vacancy Report

This memo provides an overview of current vacancies on the City of Reno's boards and commissions, as well as those anticipated within the next 90 days as of January 1, 2026. Changes in membership occurring after January 1, 2026 are not reflected.

The scope of this report is limited to internal boards and commissions for which appointments are made directly by the Reno City Council. This report's purpose is to assist the Mayor and Council in planning for upcoming appointments and reappointments, particularly to the boards where the Mayor and Council serve as Council liaisons. To further support this process, staff will also meet individually with each Council Member throughout the quarter to review vacancies and applications for the boards each Council Member recommends appointments to. These meetings will help ensure timely appointments and reappointments as vacancies occur.

fields.authors: Tyler Shaw, Agenda Manager; Cali Shy, Agenda Coordinator
fields.tldr: This memo reports current and anticipated vacancies on City of Reno boards and commissions as of January 1, 2026, covering the third quarter of 2025. Significant vacancies exist across multiple bodies, including the Access Advisory Board (6 openings), Reno City Charter Committee (9 openings), Senior Residents Advisory Board (4 openings), and Ward 4 and Ward 6 Neighborhood Advisory Boards (5–6 openings each). Staff will meet individually with each Council Member during the quarter to review vacancies and applications for boards they oversee. The report is intended to help the Mayor and Council plan timely appointments and reappointments.
fields.key_stats: Total boards/commissions covered: 18. Access Advisory Board: 6 current vacancies. Reno City Charter Committee: 9 current vacancies. Senior Residents Advisory Board: 4 current vacancies. Ward 4 Neighborhood Advisory Board: 5 current vacancies. Ward 6 Neighborhood Advisory Board: 6 current vacancies. Ward 1 Neighborhood Advisory Board: 4 current vacancies. Ward 2 Neighborhood Advisory Board: 2 current vacancies plus 3 upcoming. Ward 3 Neighborhood Advisory Board: 2 current vacancies. Ward 5 Neighborhood Advisory Board: 2 current vacancies plus 1 upcoming. Financial Advisory Board: 1 current vacancy plus 1 upcoming. Youth City Council: 3 current vacancies.
fields.key_decisions: Council must make appointments and reappointments to fill current and upcoming vacancies across 18 boards and commissions. Upcoming term expirations requiring action by March 31, 2026 include: Jillian Keller (Financial Advisory Board, February 28, 2026); Roman Schomberg (Ward 2 NAB, January 31, 2026); Stanley Dowdy (Ward 2 NAB, March 31, 2026); Patrick Fisher (Ward 2 NAB, March 31, 2026); Bryce Chisholm (Ward 5 NAB, March 31, 2026).
fields.action_items: Staff will meet individually with each Council Member throughout Q1 2026 to review vacancies and applications for boards each Council Member recommends appointments to, ensuring timely appointments and reappointments as vacancies occur.
